🧵 If you work in a SOC and you are not using the MITRE ATT&CK framework daily, you are investigating blind.  Here is a complete breakdown of what it is, why it exists, and how to use it to become a better analyst.  ───────────────────────── 🤔 WHAT IS MITRE ATT&CK? ─────────────────────────  MITRE ATT&CK (Adversarial Tactics, Techniques, and Common Knowledge) is a globally accessible knowledge base of real-world adversary behaviour - built from observed attacks against enterprise networks.  Think of it as a dictionary of how attackers actually operate - not theoretically, but based on documented intrusions from threat groups like APT28, Lazarus Group, FIN7, and hundreds of others.  It is maintained by MITRE Corporation and is completely free at attack.mitre.org.  ───────────────────────── 🗂 HOW IT IS STRUCTURED ─────────────────────────  ATT&CK is organised into three layers:  📌 TACTICS - The "why". What is the attacker trying to achieve at this stage? Examples: Initial Access, Execution, Persistence, Privilege Escalation, Defense Evasion, Credential Access, Discovery, Lateral Movement, Collection, Exfiltration, Impact.  📌 TECHNIQUES - The "how". What specific method did they use to achieve that tactic? Example: Under "Credential Access" you will find T1110 - Brute Force, with sub-techniques for Password Spraying, Credential Stuffing, and Password Cracking.  📌 PROCEDURES - The "who". Real-world examples of specific threat actors using this technique in documented attacks.  ───────────────────────── 🛠 HOW SOC ANALYSTS USE IT ─────────────────────────  ✅ Alert Enrichment - When an alert fires, map it to an ATT&CK technique. A PowerShell download cradle maps to T1059.001 (Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell). Now you know exactly which threat actors use this technique and what they typically do next.  ✅ Threat Hunting - Use ATT&CK to hunt proactively. Pick a technique your current detections don't cover - say T1003 (OS Credential Dumping) - and write SIEM queries to hunt for signs of it in your environment.  ✅ Detection Gap Analysis - Map your existing detection rules to ATT&CK techniques. Visualise the gaps using the ATT&CK Navigator (a free heatmap tool). Wherever your heatmap is empty, you are blind.  ✅ Incident Reporting - Use ATT&CK technique IDs in your incident reports. It turns vague language like "the attacker moved around the network" into precise language: "The actor demonstrated Lateral Movement via T1021.002 (SMB/Windows Admin Shares)."  ───────────────────────── 🚀 WHERE TO START ─────────────────────────  1. Visit attack.mitre.org and explore the Enterprise matrix 2. Download the ATT&CK Navigator and map your current detections 3. Pick your three biggest detection gaps and write one hunt query each 4. When your next alert fires, open ATT&CK before you close the ticket  ATT&CK does not make you a better analyst by reading it. It makes you better by using it on every single investigation.  #MITRE #ATTACKFramework #SOC #CyberSecurity #BlueTeam #ThreatHunting #InfoSec #ThreatIntel #SOCAnalyst #CyberDefense

🚨 Crypto Alert: North Korean Hackers Linked to Bitrefill Breach  Security researchers are pointing fingers at North Korea-linked actors (likely Lazarus Group) following a sophisticated breach at Bitrefill that has reportedly drained multiple user wallets. 📉🇰🇵  Bitrefill, a popular service for buying gift cards with crypto, is the latest victim in a string of high-profile "supply chain" attacks targeting the liquid crypto market.  The "How It Happened":  The Entry Point: Early forensics suggest a "poisoned" software update or a compromised admin credential allowed hackers to bypass multi-sig protections.  The Tactics: The attackers used custom "mixers" and "chain-hopping" (moving funds across multiple blockchains) to obfuscate the movement of the stolen assets within minutes.  The Suspects: The "fingerprints" left behind—specifically the use of the Lazarus-linked "AppleJeus" malware variant—strongly suggest a state-sponsored operation.  What This Means for Users:  Change Your Credentials: If you use Bitrefill, reset your password and ensure your 2FA is set to a hardware key or authenticator app (not SMS).  Revoke Permissions: Check your wallet's smart contract permissions. If you’ve granted "Unlimited Allowance" to Bitrefill’s older contracts, revoke them immediately using tools like Revoke.cash.  The "Cold Storage" Rule: This breach is a textbook reminder: Never keep more funds on a hot wallet or a third-party platform than you are willing to lose.  The Bigger Picture:North Korea is increasingly using crypto-heists to fund its state programs. As platforms get more "convenient," they often become "softer" targets for these elite hacking units. sample_tweets.text: 🚨 Exposition d'un serveur APT28 (FANCY BEAR) : une fuite historique pour le GRU Russe  En janvier 2026, des chercheurs en threat intelligence (Hunt.io puis Ctrl-Alt-Intel) ont découvert un open directory sur un serveur de command-and-control (C2) d’APT28 (alias Fancy Bear / Sednit / Forest Blizzard / GRU Unit 26165).  Les faits clés - IP exposée: 203.161.50.145   - Hébergé sur un VPS Namecheap (Phoenix, États-Unis)   - Port : 8889 (SimpleHTTPServer Python)   - Découvert le 13 janvier 2026 - Ce même serveur avait déjà été publiquement attribué à APT28 par le CERT-UA en octobre 2024! Le groupe l’utilisait encore malgré la découverte. Erreur OpSec monumentale.  Ce qui a été exposé (61 fichiers, 52 Mo, ça parait peu mais pourtant…) - 2 800 e-mails exfiltrés (gouvernements, militaires, diplomates: focus Ukraine + Balkans/NATO)   - 240 jeux de credentials complets (login + mot de passe + secrets TOTP pour contourner la 2FA)   - 11 500 contactes issus des carnets d’adresses des victimes   - 140 règles de forwarding silencieuses créées dans les boîtes mail (redirection invisible vers les attaquants)   - Payloads XSS avancés (injection CSS, vol de credentials Chrome, extraction TOTP Roundcube)   - Outils complets : serveur Flask, implants Go, historique bash des opérateurs, payloads Roundcube/SquirrelMail/Zimbra/MDaemon   - Preuves d’exploitation de CVE-2023-43770 (Roundcube)  Campagnes révélées - Operation Roundish (Hunt.io : 11 mars 2026)   - Operation RoundPress (ESET)   - Chaînes de phishing ClickFix + faux reCAPTCHA déjà signalées par CERT-UA    Ciblage prioritaire : forces aériennes roumaines, ministères de la Défense, think tanks, énergie, télécoms, santé en Europe de l’Est.  Sources principales - Hunt.io « Operation Roundish » (11 mars 2026)   - Ctrl-Alt-Intel « FancyBear Exposed » (mi-mars 2026)   - CERT-UA (attribution 2024 confirmée)  C’est rare de voir les « entrailles » d’une opération GRU aussi clairement exposées. Un cadeau pour la threat intelligence mondiale… et un rappel brutal que même les services russes font des fautes basiques d’OpSec.

🚨 Tweet #35:  This document is more explosive than anticipated. One critical detail changes everything.  Georgia Tech produced the Fancy Bear/APT28 attribution analysis before the DARPA contract existed - on their own volition, unpaid - specifically to win the contract.   The Russia attribution was essentially a job application.  How this all started: August 7, 2016 Georgia Tech produces a document titled:  "Fancy Bear/APT28 Attribution Analysis."  Fancy Bear is Russian Military Intelligence and APT28 is their hacking unit.  This is the attribution report that pointed the finger at Russia for the DNC hack.  Here is what the document reveals about how it was produced:  Georgia Tech did this research before the DARPA contract existed.  Word for word from the document:  "The research conducted in this report was done of their own volition and was not paid for by DARPA."  They produced it to show DARPA what they could do. It was a sales pitch. Georgia Tech auditioned for the contract by producing a Russia attribution report.  Then they got the contract.  Then the Obama administration used that attribution to justify everything that followed.  The Russia narrative that dominated four years of American politics.   -The Mueller investigation. -The impeachment attempts. -The surveillance of election infrastructure.  All of it traces back to an unpaid audition piece produced by university researchers competing for a government contract.  And the document confirms it was produced specifically to highlight, word for word:  "The capabilities of their technical approach."  -Not to find the truth. -To win the business, or a grant. 이란 정부가 해킹·개인정보 유출·살인 선동으로 반대자를 위협하던 4개 '가짜 해커' 사이트를 미국이 법적으로 압수·차단.  미국 법무부와 FBI가 이란 정보성(MOIS)이 운영하는 4개 웹사이트/도메인을 법원 영장으로 압수했다. 이 사이트들은 이란 정부가 지원하는 'faketivist'그룹으로, 해킹 + 심리전 + 초국가적 억압을 위해 사용됨.  ▪︎ 압수된 4개 도메인 Justicehomeland.org → Homeland Justice 그룹 관련 Handala-hack.to → Handala Hack 그룹 (주요) Karmabelow80.org → Karma Below 그룹 Handala-Redwanted.to → Handala 관련 또 다른 사이트  이제 이 사이트들은 들어가면 DOJ/FBI 경고 페이지로 바뀌어 있다. ("이 도메인은 압수됐습니다")  이 사이트들은 해킹을 자랑하고 주장 (예: 2026년 3월 미국 의료 기술 회사 Stryker에 대한 파괴적 맬웨어 공격 — Handala-hack.to에서 자랑함)했고,   비판자·반대자·이스라엘인·언론인의 개인정보 유출·공개 (이름, 사진, 주소 등)  살인·폭력 선동 (저널리스트, 반체제 인사, IDF 관련자 살해 촉구)  프로파간다 퍼뜨리기 (이란 정권 지지 + 미국·이스라엘 비난)  익명 해커처럼 위장 (Anonymous 스타일 흉내 내서 "우리는 독립 해커"처럼 보이게 함)  Handala Hack은 특히 이스라엘 관련 타겟 (IDF 190명 정보 유출, 전 총리 Bennett 휴대폰 해킹 주장 등) 많이 공격했음.  DOJ는 이것을 이란 정부의 '사이버 지원 테러'로 규정하고, 온라인 증오·폭력 선동을 막기 위한 직접 행동이라고 본다.  (원문: justthenews.com/politics-pol… DOJ 공식: justice.gov/opa/pr/justice-d…)

선관위 해킹 취약 발표로 국정원·선관위 정면충돌   - 국정원 "취약점 과장" vs 선관위 "실제 위협 존재"  선관위가 최근 공개한 해킹 취약점 발표를 두고 국정원과 선관위가 정면으로 충돌했다. 선관위는 선거 시스템 보안 취약점을 공개하며 외부 공격 가능성을 경고했다. 반면 국정원은 해당 취약점이 과장됐으며 실제 해킹 위협 수준이 낮다고 반박했다.  이 갈등은 국가 안보와 선거 공정성 문제로 확대되고 있다. 선관위 측은 취약점 증거 자료를 다수 제시하며 시스템 강화 필요성을 강조했다. 국정원은 이를 반박하며 내부 평가 결과 안정적이라고 주장했다.  코스피 갤러리 원본 게시물에 첨부된 12장 이미지에는 관련 뉴스 스크린샷과 공식 자료가 포함됐다. 이 이슈는 국가 안보 측면에서 중요한 사안으로, 보수 진영에서는 선거 보안 강화와 외부(특히 중국 등) 위협 차단을 요구하는 목소리가 높아지고 있다. gall.dcinside.com/board/view…

'구매자 책임(buyer beware)' 경고문을 붙여서요.  마이크로소프트 보안 설계자는 승인 소식에 레오나르도 디카프리오 밈을 올리며 "BOOM SHAKA LAKA"라고 축하했고요.  이번 주에 미 국방부가 앤스로픽을 '공급망 리스크'로 블랙리스트에 올렸잖아요. 앤스로픽의 죄목은 군사 목적 사용에 윤리 기준을 세운 것이고요.  마이크로소프트는 보안 검증도 못 해준 클라우드를 승인받았어요.  공급망 리스크를 걱정하는 기준이 뭔지, 솔직히 좀 헷갈리더라고요.  🔗 arstechnica.com/information-… #AI #사이버보안 @Microsoft @AnthropicAI

Google Threat Intelligence just exposed "DarkSword", an iOS full-chain exploit active since November 2025.  What makes this different: it's being used by MULTIPLE threat actors simultaneously.  Commercial spyware vendors AND state-sponsored groups.

URGENT iOS Security Alert for Every iPhone & iPad User – Especially Crypto Holders  ▫️ Google’s Threat Intelligence Group has uncovered a powerful new exploit chain called DarkSword.  This is a system-level, full-device compromise that lets attackers silently take over your iPhone or iPad simply by visiting certain compromised (but seemingly normal) websites. No extra clicks or downloads required in many cases.  It chains together six different vulnerabilities (including three zero-days) to break out of the browser sandbox, gain kernel access, and steal everything: messages, photos, passwords, location data, app contents, and - critically - any crypto wallet seeds, private keys, or balances stored or accessible on the device.  ▫️ The malware families deployed (GHOSTBLADE, GHOSTKNIFE, and GHOSTSABER) can exfiltrate data quickly and then wipe their traces, making detection extremely difficult.   Multiple threat actors - including suspected state-sponsored groups - have been actively using this since late 2025, targeting users in countries like Ukraine, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Malaysia, and beyond through watering-hole attacks on legitimate-looking sites.  ➡️  Exactly which devices are impacted?  Any iPhone or iPad running iOS or iPadOS versions 18.4 through 18.7 (inclusive). Devices on older or newer versions are not affected by this specific chain.  ▫️ The excellent news:  Apple has already patched every single vulnerability involved. The fixes landed in iOS/iPadOS 26.3 (and most earlier), with the current stable release being iOS 26.3.1.  ➡️ Can EVERY affected device update easily and safely to the protected version?  Yes - for the overwhelming majority of users. ▫️ If your iPhone or iPad is currently on iOS 18.4–18.7, simply go to Settings → General → Software Update. The update process is completely over-the-air, free, signed by Apple, and thoroughly tested for stability and safety. No computer or technical skills needed. Apple has been pushing iOS 26 heavily to everyone who can run it (iPhone 11 and newer models).  Most devices that support iOS 18 can jump straight to iOS 26.3.1 with zero issues.    Even the very oldest models that can’t upgrade to the major iOS 26 version (specifically iPhone XR, XS, and XS Max) continue to receive critical security patches on the iOS 18 branch - so they can still get protected via the latest available iOS 18.x update.    Always do a quick iCloud backup first as a precaution (takes 2–5 minutes), but millions of people update daily without problems.  ⚠️ If for any reason an update isn’t possible right now (travel, low battery, etc.), enable Lockdown Mode immediately: Settings → Privacy & Security → Lockdown Mode. It adds powerful extra protections against exactly this kind of advanced exploit (though it disables some conveniences like certain web features).Extra steps every crypto user should take right now:  Review and tighten all app permissions in Settings — revoke anything you don’t actively use.   Enable hardware-key or app-based 2FA everywhere (especially on exchanges and wallets).    Never click unknown links or visit untrusted sites.    For serious holdings, move seed phrases and large amounts to a hardware wallet and keep your phone clean.  This isn’t hype - it’s an active, in-the-wild threat that can drain wallets in minutes once your device is owned. With millions of people still on iOS 18.x, the window is closing fast.  Do this today. Update your device, double-check your security settings, and forward this to every iOS user you know - friends, family, fellow degens. One quick update could save everything.Stay safe, stay sovereign, and keep your keys cold.

Hacking gangs now routinely use tools called EDR killers to shut down the security software protecting company networks before launching ransomware attacks. These tools exploit flaws in legitimate drivers or abuse anti-virus removal software to silently disable protection — the computer shows no warning that its defences have been turned off. Researchers tracking 90 different EDR killers say the tools are now so common and easy to use that even low-skill criminals can deploy them. The records these attacks target — your payroll details, medical files, and financial data — sit behind the protection these tools are designed to destroy. National CERT warns iPhone users in Pakistan of rising cyber threats, advises immediate precautions.  CERT emphasized that iPhone users should keep their devices updated and avoid using outdated iOS versions. Users are also advised to refrain from opening suspicious or unknown links, avoid installing unauthorized VPNs, and stay away from unsecured Wi-Fi networks. Regular device restarts and attention to security alerts are recommended as part of routine safety practices.  The alert comes as the region experiences increased tension, leading to a rise in cyberattack risks. CERT warned that government, defense, and financial institutions may be targeted, highlighting threats such as deepfake campaigns, spear-phishing, and DDoS attacks.  Officials also noted the possibility of hacks targeting official social media accounts and ransomware attacks on banking and financial systems. All users, including government employees and the public, are urged to exercise caution while using emails, links, and mobile apps, and to enable multi-factor authentication (MFA) for added protection. ‼️ A cyberattack affecting Intoxalock is causing drivers to be unable to start their cars. The breathalyzer devices, known as ignition interlock systems, require drivers to blow into them to check their breath alcohol level before starting their cars, and if their blood alcohol is 0.02 or higher, the vehicle won't turn on.  However, since the cyberattack began on March 14, the company's servers have been knocked offline, meaning even sober drivers can't start their vehicles if their device is due for calibration.  The outage is affecting drivers across 46 states, with roughly 150,000 people using the system nationwide. Intoxalock says hackers have been flooding its servers, and the company has offered towing reimbursement to stranded drivers, though there's no clear timeline for when things will be fixed.  learn.intoxalock.com/status?…

‼️🇺🇸 Tampa Smoke Shop Owner Arrested in Dark Web Narcotics Case  Ryan Perez, a Spring Hill resident who owned two "Hippie Crib" smoke shops in Tampa, was arrested after a year-long investigation into allegations that he used cryptocurrency to purchase narcotics through the dark web and sold them in the Tampa Bay area.  The case gained momentum after a man in Pasco County died of an overdose on December 26, 2025. The victim's wife identified the person who supplied her husband with MDMA (ecstasy) and told deputies he had complained about feeling extremely hot the night before his death. Investigators traced the drug supply chain through a series of suppliers, ultimately leading back to Perez as the source.  According to Hernando County Sheriff Al Nienhuis, the MDMA linked to the overdose death was roughly two and a half times stronger than what is typically found on the street.  When authorities executed a search warrant at Perez's home, they reported finding:  ▪️2 pounds of MDMA ▪️10 counterfeit Adderall pills ▪️14 ½ grams of LSD ▪️2 pounds of psilocybin mushrooms ▪️1 pound of marijuana plants ▪️20 pounds of other types of marijuana  Perez was charged with trafficking MDMA, trafficking LSD, possession of mushrooms with intent to sell, and additional drug-related offenses.  His wife, Adina Perez, was also arrested on MDMA trafficking charges. A third person at the home was arrested for felony marijuana possession.  The DEA also obtained warrants for the two Hippie Crib shops, where agents reportedly seized firearms and cryptocurrency machines (???).  A DEA agent stated that Perez had been importing chemicals from China, India, and European countries, packaging them, and distributing products whose contents, including synthetic opioids and cannabinoids, were unknown to buyers.

Investigators traced cryptocurrency payments, identified buyers across the globe, and launched prosecutions, including a Bavarian father convicted after paying just €20.  An international arrest warrant has been issued for the 35-year-old operator. Investigations into 100+ individuals are still ongoing.  The message from Europol is clear: attempting to buy illegal material is enough to put you in law enforcement's crosshairs. The dark web isn't as anonymous as you think. 🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-03-21 17:29 KST
🟡 위협 점수: 5/10 (보통)

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 위협 환경은 공급망 공격(Supply Chain Attack)과 AI 에이전트 생태계를 겨냥한 공격이 급증하는 추세입니다. 특히 Trivy와 같은 오픈소스 도구의 GitHub Actions를 악용한 CI/CD 파이프라인 침해 사례가 두드러지며, 공개된 취약점(CVE)이 20시간 이내에 무기화되는 등 공격 속도가 매우 빨라졌습니다. 또한, 랜섬웨어 그룹은 EDR 우회 기술을 고도화하고 있으며, AI 기반의 피싱 및 자동화된 공격 도구들이 실무 보안 운영에 새로운 도전 과제를 제시하고 있습니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
- **Interlock Ransomware**: Cisco 방화벽 취약점을 사전 확보하여 이중 협박(Double-extortion) 공격 수행.
- **Larva-26002**: MS-SQL 서버를 타겟으로 하여 ICE Cloud 스캐너를 설치하고 제어권 탈취.
- **Russian Intelligence Services**: Signal, WhatsApp 등 암호화 메신저 사용자를 대상으로 한 피싱 캠페인 전개.
- **ShinyHunters**: Telus Digital 침해 사고를 통해 대규모 데이터 유출 시도.
- **Void Manticore (Handala Hack)**: 이란 연계 그룹으로, 의료 기술 기업(Stryker) 대상 와이퍼 공격 및 파괴적 활동 수행.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
- **ASEC 보고서**: 국내 타겟 APT 공격 동향 모니터링 결과, 2026년 2월 기준 국내 기업 및 기관 대상 공격 지속 확인.
- **CipherForce**: 대한민국 취업 포털을 대상으로 한 사이버 공격 주장.
- **기타**: 한국사회보장정보원 정보화사업 설명회 개최 등 보안 강화 움직임 포착.

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
- **CVE-2026-22557**: Ubiquiti UniFi Network Application, CVSS 10.0(Max), 인증되지 않은 경로 탐색을 통한 관리자 권한 탈취 가능.
- **CVE-2026-33017**: Langflow, Critical, 공개 후 20시간 내 악용 사례 보고됨.
- **CVE-2026-21992**: Oracle Identity Manager/Web Services Manager, Critical, 인증되지 않은 RCE 취약점.
- **CVE-2026-3888**: Ubuntu Desktop(snapd), LPE(Local Privilege Escalation)를 통한 Root 권한 획득 가능.
- **CVE-2026-20131**: Cisco FMC, Zero-day, Interlock 랜섬웨어 그룹이 사전 악용.

### 5. IOC 요약
- **CVE**:
    - CVE-2026-33017 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/03/critical-langflow-flaw-cve-2026-33017.html))
    - CVE-2026-21992 ([BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/oracle-pushes-emergency-fix-for-critical-identity-manager-rce-flaw/))
    - CVE-2026-3888 ([Qualys Blog](https://blog.qualys.com/category/vulnerabilities-threat-research))

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
- **CI/CD 파이프라인 점검**: Trivy 등 오픈소스 스캐너 및 GitHub Actions 설정 내 하드코딩된 Secret 값 즉시 교체 및 권한 최소화.
- **패치 적용**: Oracle Identity Manager(CVE-2026-21992) 및 Langflow 최신 보안 업데이트 즉시 적용.
- **네트워크 경계 방어**: Cisco 방화벽 및 네트워크 장비의 취약점(CVE-2026-20131 등) 노출 여부 확인 및 펌웨어 업데이트 수행.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
- **점수**: 8.5/10
- **근거**: 공급망 공격의 확산과 신규 취약점의 즉각적인 무기화가 기업 인프라에 실질적인 위협을 가하고 있음.

📈 일일 한도: 16,182 / 1,000,000 사용 (1.6%) | 잔여 983,818개
